headline: Union Home Minister and Minister of Cooperation Shri Amit Shah performs Bhoomi Pujan for the world’s largest curd manufacturing plant under the Amul Bengal Dairy Project in Kolkata
fullText:
Ministry of Cooperation

Union Home Minister and Minister of Cooperation Shri Amit Shah performs Bhoomi Pujan for the world’s largest curd manufacturing plant under the Amul Bengal Dairy Project in Kolkata

Under Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi’s leadership, the resolve to build a prosperous, secure, educated and culturally enriched “Sonar Bangla” is being translated into action

Bhoomi Pujan for the world’s largest curd manufacturing plant held in Bengal today; the plant will process 10 lakh litres of milk and benefit 1.20 lakh milk-producing livestock rearers

By providing 30 acres of land to Amul free of cost, Shri Suvendu Adhikari has enabled this major plant to become a reality and will help livestock rearers secure the highest possible price for their milk

From Kashmir to Kanyakumari and Gujarat to Bengal, the mishti doi made in Bengal will now be enjoyed in households across the country

Such is the strength of cooperation that for every rupee spent by an Amul consumer, 87 paise goes directly into the accounts of livestock rearers

The Government of India will also establish an initiative for animal breed improvement in Bengal, with a Sex-Sorted Semen facility

Benefits of more than 70 farmer-welfare schemes of the Ministry of Cooperation will reach Bengal’s farmers within six months

Under the “Sarathi” concept, an MoU has been signed to connect Bengal’s taxis with Bharat Taxi, enabling every Sarathi to become an owner of both the vehicle and Bharat Taxi

Bengal has abundant water and fertile land — over the next 15 years, every livestock-rearing household will have at least two animals of breeds yielding 25 per cent more milk

19 JUL 2026 9:02PM by PIB Delhi

Advancing Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi’s vision of “Sahkar Se Samriddhi” and the goal of “White Revolution 2.0”, Union Home Minister and Minister of Cooperation Shri Amit Shah today performed the Bhoomi Pujan for the world’s largest curd manufacturing plant under the Amul Bengal Dairy Project at a ceremony held at the New Town Convention Centre in Kolkata. West Bengal Chief Minister Shri Suvendu Adhikari, Speaker of the Gujarat Legislative Assembly Shri Shankarbhai Chaudhary, West Bengal Minister of Cooperation Shri Swapan Dasgupta, State Finance Minister Shri Tapas Roy, Minister of Industry and Commerce Dr Kalyan Chakraborty, Minister of Food Processing and Horticulture Shri Sharadwat Mukherjee, Chief Secretary of West Bengal Shri Manoj Kumar Agrawal, Chairman of Amul Dairy Shri Ashok Chaudhary, several dignitaries and representatives of the cooperative sector were present on the occasion.

Addressing the gathering, Union Home Minister and Minister of Cooperation Shri Amit Shah said that following the victory in the Bengal Assembly elections, he had come on behalf of Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi to sweeten the occasion for the people of Bengal with mishti doi. He said that the change brought about by the people of Bengal was distinct from, and historic in comparison with, all Assembly elections held in the State since Independence.

Shri Amit Shah said that West Bengal has been a State that inspired almost every sphere of national life. For several decades, West Bengal remained ahead of the rest of the country in education. Bengal also produced one great freedom fighter after another during the freedom struggle. In the spheres of faith, devotion and sankirtan, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, Swami Vivekananda, Ramakrishna Paramahansa and Maharshi Aurobindo established an extraordinary tradition. When it came to patriotism and dedication, Bengal, through great freedom fighters such as Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose and Khudiram Bose, never hesitated to make the supreme sacrifice.

The Union Home Minister and Minister of Cooperation said that it was in this Bengal that Gurudev Rabindranath Tagore earned global recognition, Acharya Jagadish Chandra Bose achieved major scientific breakthroughs, and Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay gave a new direction to cultural nationalism by composing “Vande Mataram” in “Anandamath”. He said that, unfortunately, Bengal remained influenced by a foreign ideology for a long period and, after emerging from it, became trapped in an ideological vacuum, corruption and crime. As a result, the dream of “Sonar Bangla” envisioned by Gurudev Rabindranath Tagore gradually faded.

Shri Amit Shah said that following Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi’s appeal and a decade-long struggle, and after the journey from 2016 to 2026, the movement for “Sonar Bangla” has once again been revived under the leadership of Shri Suvendu Adhikari. He assured the people of West Bengal that work has begun, under Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi’s leadership, to translate into reality every promise made to the people and the resolve for “Sonar Bangla” placed before them. This resolve will pave the way for building a prosperous, educated, secure and culturally enriched West Bengal.

The Union Home Minister and Minister of Cooperation said that today marks an extremely important day for the farmers and livestock rearers of West Bengal. Amul has performed the Bhoomi Pujan for the world’s largest curd manufacturing plant here. In the first phase of the project, 10 lakh litres of milk will be processed every day, benefiting around 1.20 lakh small milk producers and livestock rearers.

Shri Amit Shah said that he had earlier requested the State Chief Minister that if two plots of 15 acres each were provided to Amul, the entire system for milk production, processing and marketing for eastern India could be operated from West Bengal. He said that the proposal was earlier rejected on the ground that Gujarat would not be allowed to enter West Bengal, even though the project was not for Gujarat but for the welfare of West Bengal’s livestock rearers and milk producers.

The Union Home Minister and Minister of Cooperation said that the change in the State is now clearly visible. At his request, Chief Minister Shri Suvendu Adhikari decided to provide 30 acres of land free of cost to the Amul Federation. This decision has paved the way for nearly 100 major investments to come to West Bengal. It will also help secure the highest possible price for the milk supplied by around 2.5 lakh livestock rearers.

Shri Amit Shah expressed confidence that from Kashmir to Kanyakumari and from Gujarat to Bengal, the mishti doi produced at the Amul Bengal Dairy Project would be consumed in households across the country, and people nationwide would bless West Bengal. He said that the cooperative movement has immense capacity to organise people, economically empower them and connect them with prosperity.

Citing Amul as an example, the Union Home Minister and Minister of Cooperation said that the organisation, which is operating on such a large scale, has today become the world’s No. 1 cooperative institution. As many as 36 lakh women are associated with Amul. He said that this is the greatest example of the strength of cooperation and its economic potential.

Shri Amit Shah said that more than 18,600 rural dairy cooperative societies are currently associated with Amul, and Amul products reach almost every State in the country. Addressing every citizen who uses Amul products, he said that when a consumer spends Rs 100 on an Amul product, around Rs 87 goes directly to the farmer and livestock rearer.

He said that this money does not go to an industrialist or a large business house. Nearly 87 per cent of Amul’s total turnover is deposited directly and transparently into the bank accounts of livestock rearers. This is the true cooperative model, under which the person who produces the product receives the largest share of the business benefit.

The Union Home Minister and Minister of Cooperation said that he had requested Chief Minister Shri Suvendu Adhikari to provide additional land for the expansion of Amul and the cooperative dairy sector. The Government of India will establish a major centre for animal breed improvement in West Bengal. This will enable milk producers to access improved breeds of animals with higher milk yields. A Sex-Sorted Semen facility will also be established in the State, helping increase the number of milch animals and improve dairy productivity.

Shri Amit Shah said that, with the objective of bringing prosperity to Bengal’s farmers through the cooperative movement, he had written a letter to Chief Minister Shri Suvendu Adhikari. He said that he himself was surprised by the Chief Minister’s speed of execution. His email was received in the evening, and by 9 p.m. the same day, the West Bengal Government had appointed a Liaison Officer and responded that the officer would coordinate with the Ministry of Cooperation.

The Union Home Minister and Minister of Cooperation said that Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi has entrusted the Ministry of Cooperation with more than 70 schemes related to farmers’ welfare. Owing to the effective coordination mechanism created by Chief Minister Shri Suvendu Adhikari, the benefits of all these schemes will reach the farmers of West Bengal within the next six months.

Shri Amit Shah said that the office of the Registrar of Cooperative Societies in the State has also been fully computerised today. The work had remained pending for a long time, but the new government completed it in just nine weeks. Representatives of cooperative institutions will now be able to access the services of the Registrar’s office through their mobile phones. This will enhance transparency, accountability, efficiency and speed in the functioning of the cooperative sector.

The Union Home Minister and Minister of Cooperation said that during the programme, foundation stones were laid for 10 cooperative warehouses and warehouses with a total capacity of 1,400 metric tonnes were also inaugurated. These warehouses will provide farmers and cooperative institutions with facilities for the safe storage of their produce and help reduce distress sales at low prices.

Shri Amit Shah said that another significant new beginning has been made in West Bengal today. At present, taxi drivers are exploited by several private aggregator companies. Through “Bharat Taxi”, the Central Government has introduced the concept of the “Sarathi”. Under this arrangement, a vehicle operator will not remain merely a driver, but will also be an owner and shareholder of both the vehicle and the company. He said that a Memorandum of Understanding was signed today to connect West Bengal’s taxis with “Bharat Taxi”. As a result, no Sarathi in West Bengal will remain merely a driver; each will become a shareholder in the company, and a share of the profits earned by the company will be transferred directly to his bank account. This arrangement will provide taxi drivers with dignity, ownership and economic security.

The Union Home Minister and Minister of Cooperation said that West Bengal possesses immense potential in agriculture and animal husbandry. In several parts of Gujarat, water once had to be drawn from a depth of 1,200 feet for irrigation and drinking purposes, whereas West Bengal enjoys the special blessings of Maa Ganga. In most places here, adequate water is available at a depth of just 100 to 200 feet. The State has fertile land and limitless potential for the expansion of animal husbandry.

Shri Amit Shah said that, unfortunately, these possibilities were not fully utilised earlier. On behalf of Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi and Chief Minister Shri Suvendu Adhikari, he assured all livestock rearers in West Bengal that efforts would be made over the next 15 years to ensure that every family in the State has at least two milch animals and that the milk produced by these animals is procured through dairy cooperative institutions. He said that livestock rearers would be provided improved breeds of animals capable of producing at least 25 per cent more milk than at present. This will make animal husbandry a regular and reliable source of income for rural families and pave the way for a new White Revolution in West Bengal.

The Union Home Minister and Minister of Cooperation said that he had been in West Bengal for the past three days. During this period, several important steps had been taken to make Bengal infiltration-free, prevent fresh infiltration, improve law and order, and ensure the safety of mothers and sisters. Shri Amit Shah said that he had closely observed Chief Minister Shri Suvendu Adhikari’s working style and speed of execution and was confident that it would not take long for West Bengal to regain its strength. He assured the people of the State that the government would fulfil all their expectations and would certainly accomplish the task of realising Gurudev Rabindranath Tagore’s dream of “Sonar Bangla”.

Amul is establishing the Amul Bengal Dairy Project at the Howrah Food Park in West Bengal with an investment of approximately Rs 700 crore. Once fully operational, the project will be capable of processing around 30 lakh litres of milk per day. The plant will have the capacity to produce approximately 1,000 metric tonnes, or 10 lakh kilograms, of curd and other cultured dairy products every day. With this capacity, it will be the world’s largest curd manufacturing plant. The project’s proposed daily production capacity includes 10 lakh litres of packaged milk, two lakh litres of UHT milk, one lakh litres of ice cream, 20 metric tonnes of paneer, 10 metric tonnes of ghee and 10 metric tonnes of sweets. In addition, the plant will be capable of producing around 2.5 lakh packs of flavoured milk every day. The Amul Bengal Dairy Project will expand modern processing, value addition, cold-chain and marketing facilities in West Bengal’s dairy sector. It will provide milk-producing farmers and livestock rearers with a better, transparent and stable market for their produce and help increase their incomes. The project will play an important role in strengthening dairy cooperatives in West Bengal, creating new employment and income opportunities in the rural economy, and ushering in a new chapter of the White Revolution in the State.

(Release ID: 2286396)

### OVERVIEW  
Union Home Minister and Minister of Cooperation Shri Amit Shah performed the Bhoomi Pujan for the world’s largest curd manufacturing plant under the Amul Bengal Dairy Project in Kolkata, aiming to process 10 lakh litres of milk daily and benefit 1.20 lakh milk producers. The project aligns with Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi’s “Sahkar Se Samriddhi” vision and “White Revolution 2.0” goals, with West Bengal Chief Minister Shri Suvendu Adhikari providing 30 acres of land free of cost to enable the initiative.  

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS  
- **Event Date**: Bhoomi Pujan conducted on 19 July 2026 in Kolkata.  
- **Daily Processing Capacity**: 10 lakh litres of milk.  
- **Beneficiaries**: 1.20 lakh small milk producers and livestock rearers; potential to secure higher prices for 2.5 lakh livestock rearers.  
- **Land Provided**: 30 acres allocated free of cost by West Bengal Chief Minister Shri Suvendu Adhikari.  
- **Additional Initiatives**:  
  - Sex-Sorted Semen facility for animal breed improvement.  
  - Over 70 farmer-welfare schemes under the Ministry of Cooperation to reach Bengal’s farmers within six months.  
  - “Sarathi” concept MoU linking Bengal’s taxis with Bharat Taxi for vehicle ownership.  
- **Long-term Goals**:  
  - Every livestock-rearing household in Bengal to have at least two high-yield animals within 15 years.  
  - Nationwide distribution of mishti doi (sweet yogurt) from Kashmir to Kanyakumari.  
- **Economic Impact**: 87 paise of every rupee spent by Amul consumers directly reaches livestock rearers.

headline: Gati Shakti Vishwavidyalaya Holds 4th Convocation; 121 Students Graduate Across B.Tech. and MBA Programmes
fullText:
Ministry of Railways

Gati Shakti Vishwavidyalaya Holds 4th Convocation; 121 Students Graduate Across B.Tech. and MBA Programmes

GSV Confers First-Ever Honorary Ph.D. on E. Sreedharan for Outstanding Contributions to India's Transportation and Infrastructure

"Movement, Momentum and Mission are Essential for Viksit Bharat": Sh. Rammohan Naidu

19 JUL 2026 8:56PM by PIB Delhi

The Fourth Convocation of Gati Shakti Vishwavidyalaya (GSV) was held on 17 July 2026, with Hon. Civil Aviation Minister of India Sh. Kinjarapu Ram Mohan Naidu as the Chief Guest and distinguished guests Sh. E. Sreedharan (popularly known as the Metro Man of India, Former MD, Delhi Metro Rail Corporation Ltd.), Dr Hemang Joshi (MP, Vadodara), Rajmata of Baroda Her Highness Shubhanginiraje Gaekwad, Sh. Samir Kumar Sinha (Secretary, Civil Aviation, Govt of India) and Sh. Sukaran Singh (CEO & MD, Tata Advanced Systems Limited).

A total of 121 students were awarded their degrees related to B.Tech., and MBA programs of study. For the first time, the university awarded an MBA degree in Metro Rail Management as well. On the occasion, GSV conferred its First-ever Ph.D. Degree on ‘Honoris Causa’ basis, to Shri E. Sreedharan for his outstanding contributions to the field of transportation and infrastructure in the country.

Gold Medals for academic excellence across various branches of study were given to Vivek Yadav, Sandeep Patel, Abhinandan Gupta, Rishav Tripathi and Suresh Sengundarmudliyar. The Outstanding Project award 2026 was given to Durgesh Kumar and Sandeep Patel, while the Best Graduating Student award of 2026 was conferred to Vivek Kumar.

Delivering the Convocation address as the Chief Guest, Shri Kinjarapu Rammohan Naidu focussed on 'Three Ms': Movement, Momentum, and Mission - which represent direction, speed, and purpose respectively. He highlighted the country’s progress in the transportation sector and in particular in the aviation sector, PM Gati Shakti etc. He emphasised the role the Maintenance, Repair and Overhaul (MRO) sector would play in creating opportunities for youth in this country. He praised the Aircraft Maintenance Engineering curriculum developed within 4 months of the Directorate General of Civil Aviation’s (DGCA’s) partnership with GSV and extended the Ministry of Civil Aviation's absolute support to GSV. He strongly complimented the University's Industry-driven approach as a benchmark template for the entire higher education sector.

Dr. E. Sreedharan graced the occasion as the Guest of Honour, and emphasised the importance of continuous learning and to have a value system consisting of punctuality, integrity and professional competency along with a right attitude to succeed in life. He encouraged the young audience to give back to the society and country and for that they must possess a good character and health.

Dr. Hemang Joshi, the Vadodara MP, said it couldn’t have been a better coincidence for students to have their degrees awarded on the same day as India has launched its first indigenously developed Hydrogen-Train by Hon’ble PM. He praised the university’s 100% placement record and asked students to make the most of this degree earned, for this is not a destination but only a milestone in their journey.

Her Highness Shubhanginiraje Gaekwad strongly advised the graduating students to remain “rooted in values” and sensitive to sustainable development, while Shri Sukaran Singh highlighted the need for robust testing ecosystems and supportive regulations, highlighting Tata's aerospace and defence manufacturing initiatives, and encouraged graduates to "be the transformation" by embracing challenges and driving innovation. The GSV Vice Chancellor Prof. Manoj Choudhary presented the progress report of the University.

Gati Shakti Vishwavidyalaya, a Central University under the Ministry of Railways and headed by Chancellor Shri Ashwini Vaishnaw (Union Minister of Railways, Information & Broadcasting, Electronics & Information Technology), was established through an Act of Parliament on 06 Dec 2022 and is currently offering Bachelors, Masters and Doctoral level programs in Rail Engineering, Aviation Engineering & Operations, Maritime Engineering, Logistics and Supply Chain Management, Metro Rail Management, Ports & Shipping Logistics and Road & Highway Engineering etc., covering the entire transportation infrastructure and logistics sector. Besides the regular academic programs, GSV has a very big focus on executive training of officers across Railways (probationers as well as serving officers), Defence Forces (Army, Air Force, Navy) and Civil Services on domains of its specialization.

Aligning itself with National Education Policy 2020, GSV follows a demand-driven curriculum with a vision to be an “Industry-driven and Innovation-led” university to prepare superior human resources and research for the country's development. With a mission to be “focal point” of the entire ecosystem, GSV has worked on collaborations with leading institutions and industries across the world for industry-oriented curriculum, experiential learning, knowledge sharing and internships that helps prepare job-ready graduates. The university is acting as a central nodal agency for capacity building and training for PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan, having already trained more than 2.4 Lakh Officers through its curated course.

Dharmendra Tewari/ Dr. Nayan Solanki/ Ritu Raj/ Manik Sharma

(Release ID: 2286394)

headline: NAMASTE Scheme Reflects The Government's Unwavering Commitment To Ensuring That Every Sanitation Worker Is Able To Work With Dignity, Safety And Social Security; Scheme Aligns With The Vision Of 'Sabka Saath, Sabka Vikas, Sabka Vishwas, Sabka Prayas' : Union Minister Of Social Justice And Empowerment Dr. Virendra Kumar
fullText:
Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment

NAMASTE Scheme Reflects The Government's Unwavering Commitment To Ensuring That Every Sanitation Worker Is Able To Work With Dignity, Safety And Social Security; Scheme Aligns With The Vision Of 'Sabka Saath, Sabka Vikas, Sabka Vishwas, Sabka Prayas' : Union Minister Of Social Justice And Empowerment Dr. Virendra Kumar

Over 89, 000 Sewer And Septic Tank Workers, Over 2,81,000 Waste Pickers Validated Under The Scheme; Over 87,000 PPE Kits Distributed Among Sewer And Septic Tank Workers And 1,84,118 PPE Kits To Waste Pickers; Health Cards Issued To Over 76,000 Sewer And Septic Tank Workers And Over 1,04,000 Waste Pickers Across The Country.

19 JUL 2026 7:53PM by PIB Delhi

The Government of India is implementing the National Action for Mechanised Sanitation Ecosystem (NAMASTE) as a flagship initiative to ensure the safety, dignity and social security of sanitation workers while accelerating the transition towards mechanised sanitation across the country.

Jointly implemented by the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment and the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs, the scheme aims to eliminate hazardous manual cleaning of sewers and septic tanks by promoting mechanised cleaning practices, skill development and comprehensive welfare measures for sanitation workers.

Highlighting the Government's commitment to the welfare of sanitation workers, Union Minister of Social Justice and Empowerment Dr. Virendra Kumar said that the NAMASTE Scheme reflects the Government's unwavering commitment to ensuring that every sanitation worker is able to work with dignity, safety and social security. He said through mechanisation, skill development, healthcare and livelihood support, we are transforming sanitation work into a safer and more dignified profession while moving towards the vision of zero fatalities in sanitation work.

The Minister said that the Government has adopted a holistic approach that combines technology, occupational safety, health protection and entrepreneurship opportunities to improve the lives of sanitation workers and their families. He emphasized that the scheme aligns with the vision of 'Sabka Saath, Sabka Vikas, Sabka Vishwas, Sabka Prayas' by ensuring that those who contribute to public health and sanitation receive the respect, protection and opportunities they deserve.

Under the NAMASTE Scheme, sanitation workers are being identified, profiled and provided with a range of benefits including Personal Protective Equipment (PPE), health insurance, Ayushman Bharat coverage, occupational safety training, skill development, financial assistance for self-employment and access to modern mechanised sanitation equipment. Emergency Response Sanitation Units (ERSUs) are also being strengthened with advanced safety devices to ensure safe sanitation operations.

The scheme seeks to achieve zero fatalities in sanitation work, eliminate direct human contact with hazardous waste, ensure that sanitation operations are carried out through mechanised equipment, build a skilled sanitation workforce and promote entrepreneurship among sanitation workers through Self-Help Groups and other livelihood opportunities.

As part of its latest achievements, 89,915 Sewer and Septic Tank Workers and 2,81,117 Waste Pickers have been validated under the scheme. A total of 87,037 PPE kits have been distributed among Sewer and Septic Tank Workers and 1,84,118 PPE kits to Waste Pickers. Health Cards have been issued to 76,845 Sewer and Septic Tank Workers and 1,04,729 Waste Pickers, while 753 Emergency Response Sanitation Units (ERSUs) have been equipped with modern safety devices to strengthen emergency sanitation services across the country.

The Government continues to work closely with States, Union Territories, Urban Local Bodies and implementing agencies to expand mechanised sanitation services, strengthen occupational safety standards and improve the socio-economic well-being of sanitation workers.

Through sustained collaboration and community participation, the NAMASTE Scheme is contributing towards building a cleaner, safer and more inclusive India while ensuring dignity and respect for those who play a vital role in safeguarding public health.

PR-020-TAR/KV/GA

(Release ID: 2286362)

summaryText:
### OVERVIEW
The Government of India is implementing the National Action for Mechanised Sanitation Ecosystem (NAMASTE) scheme to eliminate hazardous manual cleaning of sewers and septic tanks by promoting mechanised practices, skill development, and welfare measures. Jointly managed by the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment and the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs, the initiative aims to achieve zero fatalities in sanitation work.

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS
* **Validation Statistics:** 89,915 sewer and septic tank workers and 2,81,117 waste pickers have been validated under the scheme.
* **PPE Distribution:** 87,037 PPE kits have been distributed to sewer and septic tank workers, and 184,118 PPE kits have been provided to waste pickers.
* **Health Coverage:** Health cards have been issued to 76,845 sewer and septic tank workers and 104,729 waste pickers.
* **Emergency Response:** 753 Emergency Response Sanitation Units (ERSUs) have been equipped with modern safety devices.
* **Core Objectives:** The scheme focuses on mechanised sanitation, occupational safety training, health insurance (including Ayushman Bharat coverage), financial assistance for self-employment, and the promotion of entrepreneurship through Self-Help Groups.

headline: Union Minister of Commerce and Industry Shri Piyush Goyal concludes successful visit to Spain, Belgium, Finland and Estonia; strengthens India's economic partnership with Europe
fullText:
Ministry of Commerce & Industry

Union Minister of Commerce and Industry Shri Piyush Goyal concludes successful visit to Spain, Belgium, Finland and Estonia; strengthens India's economic partnership with Europe

Shri Piyush Goyal Unveils ‘10×10×10’ Vision to Expand India–Spain Trade, Investment and Tourism Tenfold in a Decade

India Proposes New Initiatives on Skills, Industrial Collaboration and Defence Cooperation During Brussels Visit

Shri Piyush Goyal Invites Finnish Companies to Expand Manufacturing, R&D and Digital Infrastructure Investments in India

Shri Piyush Goyal Concludes Europe Visit with High-Level Engagements in Estonia to Boost Bilateral Economic Ties

Shri Piyush Goyal's visit advances India–EU FTA discussions on investment, market access and regulatory cooperation

19 JUL 2026 7:51PM by PIB Delhi

Union Minister of Commerce and Industry Shri Piyush Goyal concluded a five-day visit to Spain, Belgium, Finland and Estonia from 13–18 July 2026, accompanied by a high-level Indian business delegation. During the visit, he held multiple high-level engagements with ministers, senior government leaders, business executives and industry stakeholders across the four countries. The visit aimed to deepen India's economic partnership with Europe, expand investment linkages, strengthen technology cooperation and promote greater business-to-business engagement, while building on the momentum generated by the successful conclusion of the India–EU Free Trade Agreement (FTA) negotiations.

During his visit to Spain, Shri Goyal held productive discussions with H.E. Mr. Carlos Cuerpo Caballero, First Vice-President and Minister of Economy, Trade and Business, and H.E. Mr. Jordi Hereu Boher, Minister of Industry and Tourism. Shri Goyal proposed an annual India–Spain Joint Commission meeting with business participation, while Spain proposed establishing a dedicated "India Team" to facilitate bilateral engagement.

Both sides also agreed to advance technical discussions on interoperability between India's Unified Payments Interface (UPI) and Spain's Bizum digital payments platform. They identified significant opportunities for collaboration in automobiles, pharmaceuticals, chemicals, textiles, renewable energy, tourism and infrastructure. Discussions also covered investment facilitation, mobility of professionals and joint investments in Latin America, leveraging Spain's strategic position as India's gateway to Europe and Latin America. Shri Goyal welcomed the flagship C-295 aircraft programme under the 'Make in India' initiative and the progress made on the Migration and Mobility Partnership and Social Security Agreement.

Addressing the India–Spain Business Forum, Shri Goyal called upon businesses to forge early partnerships to maximise opportunities arising from the India–EU FTA and outlined an ambitious "10×10×10" vision to increase bilateral trade, investment and tourism tenfold over the next decade.

In Brussels, Shri Goyal co-chaired the 3rd India–EU Trade and Technology Council (TTC) Ministerial Meeting. He welcomed the adoption of the 2026–27 TTC Action Plan and the listing of 21 additional Indian fishery establishments, taking the total number of approved Indian establishments to 625. India also raised the issue of listing Indian ship recycling yards and proposed launching an India–EU dialogue on skills, education and talent mobility.

During meetings with Mr. António Costa, President of the European Council, Mr. Maroš Šefčovič, European Commissioner for Trade, Mr. Wopke Hoekstra, Commissioner for Climate, Net Zero and Clean Growth, Mr. Christophe Hansen, Commissioner for Agriculture and Food, and Mr. Bernd Lange, Chair of the European Parliament's Committee on International Trade (INTA), both sides reaffirmed their commitment to work towards the early signing and implementation of the India–EU FTA.

The discussions also covered negotiations on the Investment Protection Agreement and the Geographical Indications Agreement, cooperation on the Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM), regulatory simplification, market access for agricultural products, duty derogation for Basmati rice, steel scrap etc. Shri Goyal also proposed dedicated plug-and-play industrial clusters for Belgian companies and explored strategic defence collaboration with Thales in counter-drone technologies and electronic warfare. Both sides recognised the need for a more predictable, transparent and business-friendly regulatory environment to unlock the full potential of India–EU economic relations.

In Finland, Shri Goyal met Ms. Riikka Purra, Acting Prime Minister and Minister of Finance, and Mr. Sakari Puisto, Minister of Economic Affairs. The discussions focused on expanding cooperation in artificial intelligence, 6G, quantum technologies, clean energy, the circular economy, advanced manufacturing and innovation.

Both sides agreed that India's scale and talent, combined with Finland's technological capabilities, offer significant opportunities for co-development and co-manufacturing. Discussions also covered university partnerships, startup collaboration, talent mobility and research cooperation.

Shri Goyal also met the leadership of KONE and Nokia and encouraged both companies to significantly expand manufacturing, research and development, exports and digital infrastructure investments in India. He invited Finnish companies to establish manufacturing bases in India to serve global markets and deepen collaboration in telecom technologies, data centres, cloud computing and workforce development.

The Minister concluded his visit in Estonia on 18 July 2026, marking the first ministerial-level visit from India since the opening of India's resident Embassy in Tallinn in 2021 and underscoring the growing importance of bilateral economic relations. During discussions with Mr. Margus Tsahkna, Minister of Foreign Affairs, both sides explored cooperation in digital governance, fintech, UPI integration, e-residency, cybersecurity and emerging technologies. Estonia expressed strong support for the early implementation of the India–EU FTA and invited India to participate in the Estonia Digital Summit.

Addressing the India–Estonia Business Forum, Shri Goyal encouraged businesses from both countries to expand cooperation in cybersecurity, blockchain, clean energy and green hydrogen. Estonia highlighted its role as a gateway to the Nordic and Baltic region and expressed keen interest in partnering with India's rapidly growing digital economy. During the visit, the Minister also interacted with members of the Indian community and paid tribute at the Mahatma Gandhi statue in Tallinn.

Throughout the visit, Shri Goyal held extensive interactions with business leaders, industry associations and investors across all four countries. The discussions focused on building resilient supply chains, facilitating investments, strengthening innovation partnerships, promoting manufacturing collaboration and expanding opportunities for Indian MSMEs and startups.

The Minister encouraged European businesses to leverage India's rapidly growing economy, large domestic market, world-class digital public infrastructure, skilled workforce and stable policy environment to establish India as their preferred manufacturing, innovation and export hub.

The visit imparted fresh momentum to India's expanding economic engagement with Europe. The understandings reached during the visit are expected to strengthen technology partnerships, promote investment flows, improve market access for Indian exporters and expand cooperation in future-oriented sectors.

Abhishek Dayal/ Ishita Biswas

headline: Union Home Minister and Minister of Cooperation Shri Amit Shah inaugurates the first phase of the 'Museum of Word' in Kolkata
fullText:
Ministry of Home Affairs

Union Home Minister and Minister of Cooperation Shri Amit Shah inaugurates the first phase of the 'Museum of Word' in Kolkata

Today, this Museum of Words is giving concrete shape to Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi Ji's resolve for the cultural renaissance of the nation

This Museum of Words will serve as a powerful medium to carry the journey of India's languages, culture, and cultural renaissance to future generations

Language is shaped by words, words are shaped by emotions, and culture is shaped by language, without understanding this journey, the cultural renaissance of India cannot be complete

We have never shied away from innovation and have always embraced all that is noble and auspicious, that is why, despite countless challenges and assaults, our cultural journey stands as steadfast as the Himalayas

The Museum of Words should be expanded to encompass digital technologies and Artificial Intelligence, enabling Indian languages to evolve in line with the technologies of the future

This Museum of Words chronicles the entire journey of language—from Omkar to grammar, the Shruti and Smriti traditions, palm-leaf manuscripts, stone inscriptions, and the printing press, all the way to Artificial Intelligence

This museum is an innovative initiative to preserve India's multilingual consciousness, knowledge traditions, and collective memory through visual, auditory, and immersive experiences

This museum has accomplished the significant task of transforming the invisible word into a visual, auditory, tactile, and immersive experience

Bengal has long been a global beacon of India's cultural consciousness, time has now come for it to once again assume the role of guiding the world

Let us resolve to build a Viksit Bharat by 2047 and restore India's position as a Vishwa Guru, drawing strength from our words, our culture, and our self-confidence

Our resolve for language should be that every family becomes the first school of learning, every university emerges as a centre of original thought, every language is empowered with digital and AI capabil

19 JUL 2026 7:36PM by PIB Delhi

Union Home Minister and Minister of Cooperation Shri Amit Shah today inaugurated the first phase of the 'Museum of Word' in Kolkata, the capital of West Bengal. On the occasion, West Bengal Chief Minister Shri Suvendu Adhikari, Finance Minister Shri Swapan Dasgupta, Union Home Secretary, Union Culture Secretary, Director Intelligence Bureau (IB), and several other distinguished dignitaries were present.

Addressing the gathering, Union Home Minister Shri Amit Shah said that today is a momentous day for the languages and culture of the entire nation. Today, this Museum of Words is giving concrete shape to Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi Ji's resolve for the cultural renaissance of the nation. Shri Shah said that the expression of the culture of any nation or region is impossible without language, and language itself cannot exist without words born out of human emotions and expression. He said that unless future generations understand that language is formed by words, words emerge from emotions, culture is shaped through language, and culture finds its expression through language, the cultural renaissance of the country cannot be achieved. Shri Shah expressed confidence that this Museum of Words would serve as a powerful medium for carrying the journey of India's languages, culture, and cultural renaissance to future generations.

Union Home Minister Shri Amit Shah said that the Museum of Word beautifully depicts the entire journey of the composition of Panini's grammar. He said that the Rigveda states that noble and auspicious thoughts should continue to come to us from all directions—thoughts that are never suppressed, never obstructed, and on the basis of which we should shape and advance our social life. Shri Shah said that narrow-mindedness can never arise in our civilization because there is no place for narrowness in the very foundation of Indian thought. He added that whenever we perform Bhoomi Pujan or recite the Shanti Mantra, we pray for peace not only for humanity but also for animals, plants, and the entire universe. Therefore, he said, narrow-mindedness can never be a symbol of Indian culture.

Shri Amit Shah said that the Museum of Word will educate future generations about India's languages and culture for generations to come, introduce them to the country's rich cultural heritage, and also guide them in the way of life rooted in that heritage. He said that India has never imposed restrictions on ideas or thought. Shri Shah noted that the museum traces the entire journey of the written word—from the sacred Omkar to the origin of words and the development of grammar; from the Shrutis and Smritis to manuscripts and inscriptions; and from the printing press to the modern era. Union Home Minister said that the Museum of Words should be expanded to encompass digital technologies and Artificial Intelligence (AI), enabling Indian languages to evolve in line with the technologies of the future. He said that India has never shied away from innovation and has always embraced all that is noble and auspicious. That is why, despite countless challenges and assaults, the nation's cultural journey stands as steadfast as the Himalayas. He further said that the Museum of Words is an innovative initiative to preserve India's multilingual consciousness, knowledge traditions, and collective memory through visual, auditory, and immersive experiences.

Union Home Minister said that West Bengal is home to many museums, and he is confident that the Museum of Word will establish a prominent place for itself in a very short time. He said that every seeker of language and words who visits the museum will certainly leave with valuable knowledge and inspiration. Shri Shah said that introducing future generations to the science of words and linguistics was an extremely subtle and challenging task, but the Ministry of Culture has accomplished it successfully. He remarked that while words themselves cannot be confined, the Museum of Word has successfully captured and presented the science behind them. He said that words can be heard, spoken, lived, and passed on to future generations, and the Museum of Word will play a vital role in preserving and transmitting this legacy. Shri Amit Shah further said that through the Museum of Word, the Ministry of Culture has transformed India's intangible heritage into an experience that is visible, audible, tangible, and immersive.

Union Home Minister said that when the Museum of Word poses the question of what the world's first spoken word might have been, and the answer Omkar appears, one is naturally inclined to feel that Omkar was indeed the world's first word. He said that listening to a mother's lullabies in one's own language instantly evokes memories of one's mother. Shri Shah added that the museum houses a wide range of artefacts and manuscripts that offer a tangible and immersive experience to visitors. He said that the museum preserves numerous dialects, languages, and the folk traditions of every region of the country, narrating India's cultural journey through the medium of words. It also showcases inscriptions, copper plates, Bhojpatra manuscripts, handwritten manuscripts, and a rich collection of printed books. Union Home Minister said that after the journey from digital text to audio archives, the next milestone is to take this legacy into the era of Artificial Intelligence (AI).

Shri Amit Shah said that every gallery of the Museum of Word has been designed in a highly scientific manner. He said that the orientation space begins with the origin of language and attempts to explain its evolution through myths, lullabies, and sensory experiences. Shri Shah said that every individual has a deep emotional bond with their mother tongue. He emphasized that this attachment to one's mother tongue should become an integral part of one's character.

Union Home Minister and Minister of Cooperation Shri Amit Shah said that linguistics is a science that has evolved naturally. He noted that while language is scientific in nature, it has developed in response to human needs. Languages have evolved organically over time, and it is this natural evolution that has led to their widespread acceptance in society. Shri Amit Shah said that numerous great personalities, poets, and writers have contributed to preserving and enriching every language of the country. To honour their invaluable contributions, a dedicated gallery has been created in the Museum of Word. Union Home Minister said that the future of the country does not depend on how crowded its colleges and universities are. Rather, it depends on how many young people and adolescents visit its libraries. He appealed to the youth to make libraries an integral part of shaping their personality and intellectual growth.

Union Home Minister Shri Amit Shah said that West Bengal has long been a global beacon of India's civilizational consciousness. He said that there was a prolonged period during which attempts were made to detach Bengal from India's cultural consciousness, and that narrow and foreign ideologies diminished Bengal's ability to contribute to the nation and the world. Shri Shah said that Bengal is the land of Maharshi Aurobindo, Swami Vivekananda, Ramakrishna Paramahansa, and Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. He added that Bengal has also produced some of the country's finest scientists, eminent litterateurs, and one of India's greatest revolutionaries, Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose. He noted that Bengal had traditionally remained decades ahead of the rest of the country, guiding the nation with its intellectual and cultural leadership. Expressing confidence in Bengal's future, Shri Amit Shah said that the intellectual beacon of India's civilizational consciousness would once again awaken the world with its full strength. He said that under the leadership of Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi and Shri Suvendu Adhikari, the ongoing transformation in Bengal gives him confidence that the state will soon re-emerge as a radiant beacon of Indian consciousness. Union Home Minister further said that India cannot be fully understood without studying Maharshi Aurobindo. He noted that Maharshi Aurobindo interpreted India's past, explained its present, and envisioned its future for the coming generations. Quoting Maharshi Aurobindo, Shri Shah said that one day Bharat Mata would undoubtedly reclaim her place as the Vishwa Guru and strive to offer her very best to humanity.

Shri Amit Shah urged the younger generation to understand the power of words and language, refine their pronunciation, and internalize the principles of grammar to make their expression more effective and impactful. He said that doing so would greatly enhance their acceptance, credibility, and influence in society.

Union Home Minister Shri Amit Shah said that the entire nation is celebrating the 150th anniversary of Vande Mataram, and it is particularly significant that the Museum of Word is being inaugurated at such a historic moment. He said that when one stands on the stage and listens to the complete rendition of Vande Mataram, one truly realizes the depth of devotion and reverence that every citizen should have for Bharat Mata. Shri Shah remarked that despite the profound message embodied in the song, the country remained unaware of its full significance for nearly 76–77 years after Independence. He expressed satisfaction that today, Vande Mataram is sung in its entirety in schools across the country, describing it as a matter of great joy

Shri Amit Shah said that Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi conferred Classical Language status on Bengali in 2024. He also noted that the nation recently commemorated the 125th birth anniversary of Dr. Syama Prasad Mookerjee. Shri Shah said that it was Dr. Syama Prasad Mookerjee who had invited Gurudev Rabindranath Tagore to deliver the convocation address in Bengali at the University of Calcutta. He noted that during British rule, this was the first time that a convocation address had been delivered in an Indian language at any university in the country. He said that today, Bengal is once again moving steadily towards becoming 'Sonar Bangla'.

Union Home Minister Shri Amit Shah said that while human beings often believe they created language, he believes that it is language that has shaped humanity. He said that a person truly becomes human when emotions are awakened, and it is language that awakens those emotions. The foundation of every language lies in words—especially the words of one's own language—and the Museum of Word will play an important role in preserving and promoting this legacy. Shri Shah said that five principles are crucial for the preservation and development of languages. First, the language spoken within the family should become a child's first school. Second, a child's education should begin in the mother's tongue. Third, schools and universities should become centres of original thinking, because only then can society make meaningful contributions to innovation and creativity. He said that universities should lead the way in fostering original thought. He also emphasized that every Indian language should be made digitally and AI-enabled, thereby extending its vitality and relevance for another thousand years. Union Home Minister said that libraries and museums should not remain confined to their physical buildings. Instead, efforts should be made to ensure that the values, knowledge, and emotions they embody reach the country's youth and adolescents through dedicated outreach campaigns. Shri Amit Shah further urged every young person to learn at least one other Indian language alongside their mother tongue. He said that this would help not only in preserving one's own mother tongue but also in ensuring the long-term vitality of all Indian languages. He added that if governments, the education system, and, above all, the people of the country embrace and promote these five principles, it would make a significant contribution to the preservation, promotion, and service of India's languages.

Shri Amit Shah said that from the soil of Bengal, regarded as a beacon of Indian culture, the nation should resolve to strengthen its culture through the power of words. He said that a strong culture will inspire self-confidence, and with that self-confidence, India will realize the vision of a 'Viksit Bharat' by 2047 and fulfill its aspiration of re-establishing Bharat Mata as the Vishwa Guru. Shri Shah said that the Museum of Word will serve as the foundation for realizing this vision.

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS

headline: Union Home Minister and Minister of Cooperation Shri Amit Shah chairs a review meeting on West Bengal’s law and order situation in Kolkata, today
fullText:
Ministry of Home Affairs

Union Home Minister and Minister of Cooperation Shri Amit Shah chairs a review meeting on West Bengal’s law and order situation in Kolkata, today

Union Home Minister Shri Amit Shah issues several key directives to strengthen the state's security apparatus, judicial system and police capabilities

'Women Outreach Programme' should be conducted prioritising the security of women in rural areas

Officials were directed to establish regular dialogue with rural women and strive to raise awareness and empower them

Emphasis was laid on taking necessary steps to establish an ANTF, ATS and 'Special Investigation Agency (SIA)'—modeled after the NIA—within the state

A decision was taken for the NIA to conduct a thorough review of all terrorism and UAPA-related cases concerning the state

Illegal encroachments on government land in border areas must be removed

Union Home Minister assures adequate budgetary support for modernization of the state police and improvement of service delivery systems

One-third of the security camps established in areas formerly affected by Left-Wing Extremism will be converted into public service centres to ensure the availability of government services

Capacity building in the field of cybersecurity should be strengthened, regular NCORD meetings should be held, and a comprehensive flood management plan should be prepared for Kolkata

19 JUL 2026 7:02PM by PIB Delhi

Union Home Minister and Minister of Cooperation Shri Amit Shah chaired a review meeting on the West Bengal’s law and order situation in Kolkata, today. The meeting was attended by the Chief Minister of West Bengal, the State Chief Secretary, and senior officials from the Union Ministry of Home Affairs and the West Bengal government.

During the meeting, Union Home Minister Shri Amit Shah gave several important instructions to strengthen the state's security apparatus, judicial system and police capabilities. Prioritizing women's safety in rural areas, Union Home Minister directed the implementation of a 'Women Outreach Programme.' He emphasized the need to create awareness and empower rural women through regular interaction with officials. He also stated that the state government could directly contact the Union Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) should any need arise in this regard.

To further strengthen the state's internal security, emphasis was laid on taking necessary steps to establish an 'Anti-Narcotics Task Force (ANTF),' an 'Anti-Terrorist Squad (ATS),' and a 'Special Investigation Agency (SIA)'—modeled after the NIA—within the state. Additionally, it was decided that the National Investigation Agency (NIA) would conduct a thorough review of all terrorism and UAPA-related cases concerning the state. Directives were also issued to remove illegal encroachments on government land in the state's border areas.

Special emphasis was laid on capacity augmentation, infrastructure strengthening, and the development of state-of-the-art facilities for the West Bengal Police. Union Home Minister and Minister of Cooperation Shri Amit Shah assured that adequate budgetary support would be provided for the modernization of the state police and improvement of service delivery systems. In this process, a comprehensive review of the police force cadre will be conducted to increase its strength in line with the national average, and the number of police districts will be rationalized. Additionally, emphasis was placed on filling all vacant posts in a time-bound manner, reviewing training capacities, phasing out reliance on civic volunteers, and prioritizing the recruitment of constables.

A consensus was reached to convert one-third of the security camps established in areas formerly affected by Left-Wing Extremism into Public Service Centres to ensure the access to government services. Furthermore, directives were issued regarding capacity building in the cybersecurity sector, holding regular NCORD meetings to combat drug trafficking, and formulating a comprehensive flood management plan for Kolkata. At the end of the meeting, key priorities for immediate action were identified and the concerned agencies were directed to ensure their time-bound implementation.

headline: Department of Legal Affairs Organises 8th BRICS Heads of Prosecution Services Meeting
fullText:
Ministry of Law and Justice

Department of Legal Affairs Organises 8th BRICS Heads of Prosecution Services Meeting

20 JUL 2026 2:28PM by PIB Delhi

The Department of Legal Affairs, Ministry of Law and Justice, Government of India, under the aegis of India’s prestigious 2026 BRICS Chairship, is organising the virtual 8th Meeting of BRICS Heads of Prosecution Services from 20-21 July 2026. Presided over by the Ld. Attorney General for India, Shri R. Venkataramani, this important official dialogue brings together prosecution heads from member countries. The landmark meeting perfectly advances India's overarching BRICS vision of “Building for Resilience, Innovation, Cooperation and Sustainability.” By uniting the prosecution leadership of member countries, the meeting highlights a shared commitment to fostering stable, fair, and predictable frameworks that support economic growth across the entire BRICS bloc.

The virtual meeting will focus on the timely and highly apposite theme, “Exchanging Experiences and Best Practices in Criminal Adjudication from the Prosecution’s Perspective”. Over the two days, senior leaders will discuss modern institutional challenges and exchange vital practices of adjudication. The discussions will carry forward the digital thread initiated in previous forums, moving the concepts of Artificial Intelligence and justice into practical, day-to-day procedures. By examining technology-enabled adjudication, the meeting addresses essential areas such as the digitisation of criminal procedures, the management of electronic evidence, and the implementation of time-bound trials.

A central focus of this year's dialogue is the shared goal of making the criminal justice system more sensitive, transparent, and victim-centric. Member countries will share their unique experiences to improve prosecutorial effectiveness and build greater public trust in prosecution services. The forum acknowledges that tackling complex modern challenges such as transnational financial crimes, cyber threats, and environmental violations requires strong cross-border cooperation. Through close dialogue, the meeting aims to enhance mechanisms for mutual legal assistance and cross-border evidence sharing while promoting professional capacity building for Prosecutors.

By bridging geographical distances through digital technology, this meeting marks a significant step forward in deepening institutional bonds between BRICS nations. The Department of Legal Affairs, Ministry of Law and Justice, Government of India, remains dedicated to exploring practical, cooperative frameworks for smoother cooperation and establishing digital platforms for capacity building. Ultimately, this official meeting underscores India’s proactive leadership in shaping a modern, digitally empowered framework, ensuring a more just, efficient, and cooperative future for all member countries.

headline: TEXT OF THE OPENING REMARKS MADE IN THE HOUSE TODAY BY SHRI C. P. RADHAKRISHNAN, HON'BLE CHAIRMAN, RAJYA SABHA
fullText:
Rajya Sabha Secretariat

TEXT OF THE OPENING REMARKS MADE IN THE HOUSE TODAY BY SHRI C. P. RADHAKRISHNAN, HON'BLE CHAIRMAN, RAJYA SABHA

20 JUL 2026 1:50PM by PIB Delhi

CHAIRMAN: Hon'ble Members, on behalf of the entire house and myself I welcome the new members. I also extend a warm welcome to all of you as we begin the 271stSession of the Rajya Sabha.

This House has a proud tradition of meaningful deliberations, and I am confident this Session will uphold that legacy. We have 19 sittings before us, and it is our collective responsibility to utilize this valuable time to debate and decide on issues of national importance.

Hon'ble Members, from this Session, the Secretariat has introduced live AI-based simultaneous interpretation of the proceedings in nine languages through the Multi-Media Devices at Members' seats. This facility will gradually be expanded to all twenty-two scheduled languages. Members are requested to share their feedback for its further improvement.

This Session has an important legislative and deliberative agenda. I urge Members to remain focused on the timely disposal of business, as every disruption delays discussion on matters affecting the nation and the people we represent.

I appeal to Members across all sides of the House to uphold the dignity, discipline and decorum of this august institution. While differences of opinion are integral to parliamentary democracy, they must be expressed in a manner that preserves the sanctity of the House. Disruptions should not be allowed to undermine its functioning.

With these words, I wish you all a productive, constructive and purposeful Session.

headline: TEXT OF THE OBITUARY REFERENCES MADE BY THE HON'BLE CHAIRMAN IN THE HOUSE TODAY
fullText:
Rajya Sabha Secretariat

TEXT OF THE OBITUARY REFERENCES MADE BY THE HON'BLE CHAIRMAN IN THE HOUSE TODAY

20 JUL 2026 1:49PM by PIB Delhi

CHAIRMAN: Hon'ble Members, it is with profound sorrow that I refer to the passing away of Shriman Balbir K. Punj Ji, Shriman Swapan Sadhan Bose Ji, Shriman Rangasayee Ramakrishna Ji, Shriman H. Hanumanthappa Ji, former Members of this House and His Highness Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al-Thani, Father Amir of the State of Qatar.

Shriman Balbir K. Punj Ji, passed away on the 18th of April, 2026 at the age of 76. He was a Member of the Rajya Sabha representing the State of Uttar Pradesh, from 2000 to 2006 and Odisha, from 2008 to 2014.

Born on the 2nd of October, 1949 at Lalowal in Gurdaspur District of Punjab, Shriman Balbir Punj Ji was an eminent journalist, author, thinker and public intellectual.

He began his professional career in journalism and earned recognition for his incisive writings and analytical commentary on issues of national importance. Shriman Balbir Punj Ji also authored widely acclaimed books, reflecting his deep interest in India's civilisational heritage, public policy and national affairs.

He also served as the Chairman of the National Commission for Youth under the Government of India.

In the passing away of Shriman Balbir K. Punj Ji, the country has lost an eminent parliamentarian, a distinguished journalist and an accomplished author.

Shriman Swapan Sadhan Bose Ji passed away on the 12th of May, 2026, at the age of 78 years. He served as a Member of the Rajya Sabha representing the State of West Bengal from 2005 to 2011.

Born on the 23rd of January, 1948, at Howrah in West Bengal, Shriman Bose Ji earned widespread recognition for his distinguished contribution to the promotion and development of football and sports administration in the country.

Popularly known as 'Tutu Bose', Shriman Bose Ji was associated with the historic Mohun Bagan Athletic Club for over three decades and also served as its President. His visionary leadership, and unwavering commitment to the cause of sports earned him immense respect across the sporting fraternity. He also served as a Member of the FIFA Club Task Force, representing Indian football in international sporting forums.

In his passing, the country has lost an eminent sports administrator, an able parliamentarian, and a devoted public figure.

Shriman Rangasayee Ramakrishna Ji passed away on the 4th of June, 2026, at the age of 92 years. He served as a Member of the Rajya Sabha representing the State of Karnataka from 2012 to 2018.

Born on the 15th of March, 1934 at Chidambaram in Cuddalore District of Tamil Nadu, Shriman Ramakrishna Ji had a distinguished career in public service spanning several decades.

A member of the Indian Administrative Service (Rajasthan Cadre:1957), he held various important positions in the Government of Rajasthan and Government of India. He was also connected with the Economic Commission for Europe in Geneva and was a consultant to GATT and UNCTAD. Later, he assumed the office of Secretary General of the Association of Synthetic Fibre Industries.

In the passing away of Shriman Rangasayee Ramakrishna Ji, the country has lost a dedicated public servant and an accomplished parliamentarian.

Shriman H. Hanumanthappa Ji passed away on the 12th of July, 2026, at the age of 94 years. He served as a Member of the Rajya Sabha representing the State of Karnataka for three consecutive terms during 1982 to 2000.

Born on the 13th May, 1932, at Guddada Rangavvanna Hally village in the Chitradurga District of Karnataka, Shriman Hanumanthappa Ji actively participated in the Indian National Movement during his student days and remained associated with constructive social work throughout his public life.

An advocate by profession, he served in several important capacities, including as the Chairman of the Railway Service Commission, Bengaluru, and the National Commission for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes.

During his long public career, he worked tirelessly for rural development, irrigation, farmers' welfare and the promotion of village industries.

In the passing away of Shriman H. Hanumanthappa Ji, the country has lost an eminent parliamentarian, a dedicated social worker and a public servant par excellence.

Hon'ble Members, I also refer with profound sorrow to the passing away of His Highness Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al-Thani, Father Amir of the State of Qatar.

His Highness Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al-Thani, passed away on the 12th of July, 2026 at the age of 74. He served as the Amir of the State of Qatar for 18 years from June 1995 to June 2013.

He was a visionary leader who laid the foundation of modern Qatar and took it to great heights of development and prosperity. He also played a pivotal role in nurturing India-Qatar relations.

The House joins the bereaved family, the Government, and the people of the State of Qatar in mourning the passing away of His Highness Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al-Thani.

We deeply mourn the passing away of Shriman Balbir K. Punj Ji, Shriman Swapan Sadhan Bose Ji, Shriman Rangasayee Ramakrishna Ji, Shriman H. Hanumanthappa Ji and His Highness Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al-Thani.

I request Members to rise in their places and observe silence as a mark of respect to the memory of the departed.

(Release ID: 2286511)
summaryText:
### OVERVIEW  
The Rajya Sabha Secretariat announced the passing of four former Members of Parliament and the Amir of Qatar, with the Chairman paying tribute to their contributions.  

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS  
- **Shriman Balbir K. Punj Ji**: Died on 18 April 2026 at age 76; Rajya Sabha member for Uttar Pradesh (2000–2006) and Odisha (2008–2014); born 2 October 1949; journalist, author, and former Chairman of the National Commission for Youth.  
- **Shriman Swapan Sadhan Bose Ji**: Died on 12 May 2026 at age 78; Rajya Sabha member for West Bengal (2005–2011); born 23 January 1948; sports administrator, FIFA Club Task Force member, and President of Mohun Bagan Athletic Club.  
- **Shriman Rangasayee Ramakrishna Ji**: Died on 4 June 2026 at age 92; Rajya Sabha member for Karnataka (2012–2018); born 15 March 1934; Indian Administrative Service officer, economist, and consultant to GATT and UNCTAD.  
- **Shriman H. Hanumanthappa Ji**: Died on 12 July 2026 at age 94; Rajya Sabha member for Karnataka (1982–2000); born 13 May 1932; social worker, advocate, and Chairman of the Railway Service Commission and National Commission for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes.  
- **His Highness Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al-Thani**: Died on 12 July 2026 at age 74; Amir of Qatar (June 1995–June 2013); key figure in India-Qatar relations.  
- The Rajya Sabha observed silence as a mark of respect.

headline: APEDA Facilitates First Export of 24 MT Frozen French Fries from Uttarakhand to Iraq
fullText:
Ministry of Commerce & Industry

APEDA Facilitates First Export of 24 MT Frozen French Fries from Uttarakhand to Iraq

24 MT Frozen French Fries Export Expands Global Market Opportunities for Value-Added Agricultural Products from Uttarakhand

20 JUL 2026 1:07PM by PIB Delhi

The Agricultural and Processed Food Products Export Development Authority (APEDA), under the Ministry of Commerce & Industry, Government of India, has facilitated the first export of 24 Metric Tonnes (MT) of Frozen French Fries from Kashipur in Udham Singh Nagar district of Uttarakhand to Iraq. Exported by Virun Global Trade Pvt. Ltd., the consignment marks a significant milestone in promoting value-added agricultural exports from Uttarakhand while strengthening India's processed food export basket.

The exporter expressed confidence that the quality of the product would lead to repeat orders and long-term business relationships in international markets, creating sustained export opportunities for the company and the region. APEDA has supported the exporter in participating in leading international trade exhibitions, including SIAL, Gulfood, IndusFood and World Food India, enabling greater market outreach and buyer engagement.

India continues to strengthen its position as a global supplier of processed agricultural products. During 2025-26, India's exports of processed vegetables reached USD 932.25 million, comprising 7,26,874.70 MT. Of this, value-added potato products accounted for exports of 277,108.51 MT valued at USD 289.40 million. Processed fruits and vegetables together contributed nearly 10 per cent of APEDA's scheduled exports during the year.

Chairman, APEDA, Shri Abhishek Dev stated that logistics costs continue to influence the competitiveness of exports from landlocked states such as Uttarakhand. He said APEDA is working closely with the Government of Uttarakhand on the formulation and implementation of the State Agri Export Policy, including provisions for transport assistance to improve export competitiveness and facilitate greater market access for exporters.

The expanding processed food industry in Uttarakhand is creating stronger market opportunities across the agricultural value chain by generating sustained demand for quality raw produce, promoting value addition at source and encouraging greater participation in export-oriented supply chains. Such initiatives are expected to enhance farmers' incomes through improved market access while boosting the state's processed food exports.

APEDA continues to work closely with State Governments, exporters, Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs), processors and other stakeholders to strengthen export infrastructure, facilitate access to global markets and promote value-added agricultural exports from India.

Abhishek Dayal/ Garima Singh/ Ishita Biswas

(Release ID: 2286498)
